findings.issue: getInvitation() now uses makeRequest for the public invitation metadata endpoint. makeRequest unconditionally adds Content-Type: application/json and, when localStorage.access_token exists, Authorization: Bearer <token>. Unlike acceptInvitation(), this GET is mounted on the public unauthenticated router, so a signed-in or stale-token browser viewing an invitation leaks bearer credentials to a public lookup endpoint and turns a simple public GET into an authenticated-looking/preflighted request. The prompt's known gap covers OAuth init/redeem auto-attach; this is the invitation metadata fetch path.
findings.remediation: Allow makeRequest callers to opt out of default auth/default JSON headers, and call getInvitation(token, signal) with that public/no-auth option so /v1/invitations/:token is fetched without Authorization and without Content-Type on the bodyless GET.
